steps.title: Set the function value
steps.content:
The question asks when the function value is $73$, so replace $f(x)$ with $73$ and isolate the exponential expression by subtracting $9$ from both sides.
$$16^{\frac{x}{12}}+9=73$$
$$16^{\frac{x}{12}}=64$$

steps.title: Rewrite with base two
steps.content:
Write both sides as powers of $2$ so the exponents can be compared. Since $16=2^4$ and $64=2^6$, the equation becomes
$$(2^4)^{\frac{x}{12}}=2^6$$
$$2^{\frac{x}{3}}=2^6$$

steps.title: Equate the exponents
steps.content: The bases are equal, so the exponents must be equal. Solve the resulting linear equation for $x$. $$\frac{x}{3}=6$$ $$x=18$$ This value makes $f(x)$ equal to $73$. The answer is 18.
